text:
# Page 1^page=1
The connector enables the DTI to retrieve real time data from the customer's systems. It handles authentication with these systems and converts the data into the standardized formats used on the DTI. This document references itemId multiple times. This is the ID generally used to identify any entity for which an AASX file is being generated. For general purposes, this ID can be whatever string is more convenient to the customer. If the customer's systems already use a certain format of product ID, ERP ID or similar, these can be used. The final ID for the asset and other values will be computed from it automatically using the rules defined in the DTI. Due to these derived values and how the value is generally used, there are some restrictions on this value: Uniqueness: The value must perfectly identify a single entity Length: The value must be at least 1 and at most 2048 characters. 2048 is a theoretical upper limit, values which are too big may cause problems for certain browses or other tools which might need to read the data Prefer to stick to values of no more than 50 characters if possible The ID can only contain characters allowed on XML Schema 1.0 strings In short, it should comply with the following regex: ^[\x09\x0A\x0D\x20- \uD7FF\uE000-\uFFFD\u00010000-\u0010FFFF]*$ The ID should be URL encodable Connector Related topics Basic information Connector | DTI Documentation Version: 1.0.11 https://docs.neoception.dev/connector/index.html 1 / 28

# Page 2^page=2
For more in depth details on why these restriction exist, see the AAS Identifier type and its uses thought the AAS architecture. HTTP Method: GET Endpoint: https://your-service/health This endpoint allows the DTI check the status of the connector and customer systems, enabling detection of various kinds of issues. Anonymous access to this call is recommended. 200 OK in case the connector is operational. 5XX Server Error if the connector itself is not healthy or if it is currently unable to access customer systems (due to a connection error for example). In both cases, the contents of the response are not consumed by the DTI, only the status code. HTTP Method: GET Endpoint: https://your-service/versions Returns the version of the connector specification implemented by the service. It may also return the version of the connector application itself. Specification Health Motivation Response Versions Connector | DTI Documentation Version: 1.0.11 https://docs.neoception.dev/connector/index.html 2 / 28

# Page 3^page=3
This endpoint allows the DTI to verify which version of the connector specification is supported by the service. It can also provide the current version of the deployed connector application for operational transparency and troubleshooting. 200 OK with VersionsResponse Required field Contains the version of the connector specification implemented by the service Must follow Semantic Versioning 2.0.0 Optional field Contains the version of the connector application itself May be omitted or set to null if this information is not available Must follow Semantic Versioning 2.0.0 when provided Required field String representation of a semantic version Motivation Response VersionsResponse.spec VersionsResponse.application ServiceVersion.version public class VersionsResponse { public ServiceVersion Spec { get ; set ; } public ServiceVersion ? Application { get ; set ; } } public class ServiceVersion { public string Version { get ; set ; } } c# Connector | DTI Documentation Version: 1.0.11 https://docs.neoception.dev/connector/index.html 3 / 28

# Page 4^page=4
Must follow the Semantic Versioning 2.0.0 format, e.g. 1.0.0 , 2.3.4 , or 1.2.0- beta.1 Example response: In case of an unexpected error, the endpoint should return 500 Server Error . HTTP Method: GET Endpoint: https://your-service/product/hierarchy/levels Describes the general structure of a product/asset classification system by enumerating its hierarchy levels. The Neoception DTI provides flexible means for on demand asset administration shell �AAS� generation, able to cover the whole product/asset portfolio of large companies. Using mapping rules in the Back Office UI, a Content Admin can choose structure and content of the generated AASs. These rules need input to work on in order to efficiently reflect the complex requirements of the AAS generation for such a large product/asset portfolio. At the point in time these rules are evaluated, they work on a collection of key/value pairs, in their entirety also called "rule context". In some scenarios, the location of a product/asset within a hierarchical classification system is an extremely valuable contribution to this rule context. Each customer of the Neoception DTI is free to choose the classification system which supports a Content Hierarchy Levels Motivation { "spec" : { "version" : "1.0.0" }, "application" : { "version" : "2.3.4" } } json Connector | DTI Documentation Version: 1.0.11 https://docs.neoception.dev/connector/index.html 4 / 28

# Page 5^page=5
Admin in the best way possible to create efficient and powerful mapping rules for the required data transformation. The DTI Core just needs to know how many levels the classification hierarchy consists of and how they are named. 200 OK with List<ProductHierarchyLevel> Example response: Response public class ProductHierarchyLevel { public short Level { get ; set ; } public string ? Name { get ; set ; } } [ { "level" : 1 , "name" : "Segments" }, { "level" : 2 , "name" : "Main groups" }, { "level" : 3 , "name" : "Groups" }, { "level" : 4 , "name" : "Classes" } ] c# json Connector | DTI Documentation Version: 1.0.11 https://docs.neoception.dev/connector/index.html 5 / 28

# Page 6^page=6
HTTP Method: GET Endpoint: https://your-service/product/hierarchies The tree with the full hierarchy where products can be placed is sometimes useful for certain operations. This call is currently not in active use, but may be used on future updates. As such it may be implemented fully or left with a placeholder implementation. Note� This endpoint must be available even if not fully implemented. 200 OK with List<ProductHierarchyNode> as the body if fully implemented. 200 OK with [] body if left as placeholder. Hierarchies Motivation Response public class ProductHierarchyNode : ProductHierarchyLevel { public List < ProductHierarchyNode > Children { get ; set ; } = new (); } c# Connector | DTI Documentation Version: 1.0.11 https://docs.neoception.dev/connector/index.html 6 / 28

# Page 7^page=7
[ { "level" : 1 , "name" : "27 Electric engineering, automation, process control engineering" , "children" : [ { "level" : 2 , "name" : "27-06 Cable, wire" , "children" : [ { "level" : 3 , "name" : "27-06-01 Energy cable" , "children" : [ { "level" : 4 , "name" : "27-06-01-07 Energy cable >= 1 kV, for permanent installation" , "children" : [] } ] } ] }, { "level" : 2 , "name" : "27-28 Identification" , "children" : [ { "level" : 3 , "name" : "27-28-04 Inductive identification" , "children" : [ { "level" : 4 , "name" : "27-28-04-01 Identification, RFID Reader" , "children" : [] } ] } ] } json Connector | DTI Documentation Version: 1.0.11 https://docs.neoception.dev/connector/index.html 7 / 28

# Page 8^page=8
] }, { "level" : 1 , "name" : "28 Vehicle (complete vehicle)" , "children" : [ { "level" : 2 , "name" : "28-01 Automobile" , "children" : [ { "level" : 3 , "name" : "28-01-01 Passenger car" , "children" : [ { "level" : 4 , "name" : "28-01-01-07 Electric car (EV)" , "children" : [] } ] } ] } ] }, { "level" : 1 , "name" : "29 Home economics, Home technology" , "children" : [ { "level" : 2 , "name" : "29-15 Major domestic appliance" , "children" : [ { "level" : 3 , "name" : "29-15-01 Refrigerator, freezer (household)" , "children" : [ { "level" : 4 , "name" : "29-15-01-01 Refrigerator (household)" , "children" : [] } Connector | DTI Documentation Version: 1.0.11 https://docs.neoception.dev/connector/index.html 8 / 28

# Page 9^page=9
HTTP Method: GET Endpoint: https://your-service/model Enumerates all data source systems properties available for a Content Admin to map to the asset administration shell target structure properties. A unique list of property IDs is required to fetch specific data from the client's system related to a product. This endpoint returns a list of properties, where each property contains an ID that must be unique, a name that is optional but can provide additional description for the property, and a property type that indicates whether it is a document (such as an image, PDF, SVG, others) or a standard text property. This model also supports the Filters field, that allows adding a dictionary which enables the Admin to more precisely filter each Property in the back-office. Data Model Motivation ] }, { "level" : 3 , "name" : "29-15-03 Dishwasher, laundry appliance" , "children" : [ { "level" : 4 , "name" : "29-15-03-01 Dishwasher (household)" , "children" : [] } ] } ] } ] } ] Connector | DTI Documentation Version: 1.0.11 https://docs.neoception.dev/connector/index.html 9 / 28

# Page 10^page=10
200 OK with List<Property> Intended to serve in machine-to-machine communication only. Therefore, its readability for humans is secondary. Every property ID must be unique. A subset of these Ids will be included in the request content of the GET /product/{itemId}/values call, depending on the concrete itemId and the configured asset administration shell content. The actual Property.id seen on the UI of the DTI back-office may be slightly changed depending on if certain features are in use. This ID is case-sensitive There is no pre-established format for the product IDs Response Property.id public enum PropertyType { Property , Document } public class Property { public string Id { get ; set ; } public string ? Name { get ; set ; } public string ? Description { get ; set ; } public PropertyType Type { get ; set ; } public Dictionary < string , HashSet < string >>? Filters { get ; set ; } public List < string >? SemanticIds { get ; set ; } = new () } c# c# Connector | DTI Documentation Version: 1.0.11 https://docs.neoception.dev/connector/index.html 10 / 28

# Page 11^page=11
Intended to support a Content Admin to easily recognize the source system properties, therefore a good human readability is recommended Although not explicitly required, these names should also be unique for all properties included in the response content, otherwise a Content Admin might face duplicate entries in the UI The use of Property.name is optional but recommended, otherwise a Content Admin needs to deal with the usually rather cryptic content of Property.id In case the Connector represents more than a single data source system, the origin of each property may be indicated using e.g. a specific prefix like PIM*, ERP*, PLM_ etc. Intended to support a Content Admin to easily recognize the source system properties, therefore a good human readability is recommended Not in active use, but will appear when checking the details of properties on the backoffice UI Optional value. If present, should provide details on the nature of the property Specifies the property value content which will be returned later in the response content of the call GET product/{itemId}/values 0 indicates that the value of a property will be a primitive and scalar like e.g. a single string, integer, float, double etc. Property.name Property.Description Property.type enum PropertyType { Property = 0 , Document = 1 , } ts Connector | DTI Documentation Version: 1.0.11 https://docs.neoception.dev/connector/index.html 11 / 28

# Page 12^page=12
1 announces that the value of a property will be a document (such as an image, PDF, SVG, others). Choose this if either the document content is directly returned as base64-encoded string value or the property value is a URL referencing the document on a freely accessible server or the document is e.g. contained on a restricted server and the value of this property is just an intermediate ID/voucher/ticket for this server which needs to be resolved in a second step after GET product/{itemId}/values using POST /product/{itemId}/documents to retrieve the actual document Filters are optional key to value array objects that can be used for further filtering by the DTI. Each value should be unique within each list, but they can appear on multiple keys There are no predefined values or requirements by the DTI regarding these. They may be defined as necessary for the content admin. In general, they can be thought of as a way of adding custom values to the rule context. Filter keys are case-insensitive Intended for additional values that may be used as semantic Ids Currently not in active use. However, will be used for automated mapping via matching semantic IDs from properties to the ones in submodels templates Values must obey the same general constraints as AAS Identifiers Property.Filters Property.SemanticIds Connector | DTI Documentation Version: 1.0.11 https://docs.neoception.dev/connector/index.html 12 / 28

# Page 13^page=13
HTTP Method: GET Example response: Product Hierarchy [ { "id" : "PIM_Thumbnail" , "name" : "PIM_Thumbnail" , "description" : "Thumbnail of the product" , "type" : 1 }, { "id" : "PIM_EmailAddress" , "name" : "PIM_EmailAddress" , "type" : 0 }, { "id" : "PIM_Website" , "name" : "PIM_Website" , "type" : 0 }, { "id" : "PIM_ManufacturerProductDesignation" , "name" : "PIM_ManufacturerProductDesignation" , "filters" : { "source" : [ "PIM2" ], "department" : [ "Department1" , "Department2" ] }, "type" : 0 }, { "id" : "MarkingFile1" , "name" : "MarkingFile1" , "type" : 1 , "semanticIds" : [ "0173-1#02-BAA120#008" ] } ] json Connector | DTI Documentation Version: 1.0.11 https://docs.neoception.dev/connector/index.html 13 / 28

# Page 14^page=14
Endpoint: https://your-service/product/{itemId}/hierarchy This endpoint receives a itemId and returns the path to the location of the referenced product/asset within the hierarchical product/asset classification system as JSON in the response content. Rules can use Product Hierarchy filters and therefore, each time a request for a specific product is made we need to know where in the company hierarchy it is located to then know if a rule applies or not. For example instead of creating individual rules for 500 products in the "Groups" hierarchy, a content administrator can create a single rule that applies to all products at the "Groups" hierarchy level. 200 OK with List<ProductHierarchyLevel> on success or 404 Not Found if the provided itemId does not exist. Motivation Response public class ProductHierarchyLevel { public short Level { get ; set ; } public string ? Name { get ; set ; } } c# Connector | DTI Documentation Version: 1.0.11 https://docs.neoception.dev/connector/index.html 14 / 28

# Page 15^page=15
HTTP Method: POST Endpoint(Post): https://your-service/product/{itemId}/values This endpoint receives a itemId and the specification of the required property values for this product/asset as JSON in the content. In the context of this call, property values comprise text, numbers and documents. For textual property values like e.g. "left", "right", "low", "high" etc. and documents, language information is additionally provided. The call is expected to return the requested property values for the given asset as JSON in the response content. During the dynamic AAS generation, the DTI Core can create the target data structure with the means available in its own database. However, the intermediate result of these operations is just the skeleton of the target data structure, the still Product Values Motivation [ { "level" : 1 , "name" : "27 Electric engineering, automation, process control engineering" }, { "level" : 2 , "name" : "27-27 Sensor technology, safety-related sensor technology" }, { "level" : 3 , "name" : "27-27-09 Optoelectronic sensor" }, { "level" : 4 , "name" : "27-27-09-16 Optical distance sensor" } ] json Connector | DTI Documentation Version: 1.0.11 https://docs.neoception.dev/connector/index.html 15 / 28

# Page 16^page=16
missing parts are the concrete property values for the selected product/asset. In order to fill them in, the DTI Core asks the Connector for exactly the property values required to e.g. show a submodel in the DTI AAS Viewer or generate an AASX file. The request body contains a carefully selected subset of the propertyId values formerly retrieved using GET /model, minimizing the query effort and the resulting data traffic volume. Depending on the quality of the data mapping configuration performed by the Content Admins, this call should only request property values available for the selected product/asset. In case of a mismatch in this regard, the Connector can simply omit the property values which are not available for a specific product/asset. The Connector is also free to return more property values than requested, e.g. for simplicity all property values available for a product/asset, although in most cases the performance of this call should be improved when making use of the given propertId values in the request body to formulate more specific queries to the data source system. This endpoint is integral part of the dynamic asset administration shell �AAS� generation. It will be triggered by a call to the official AAS API of the DIT Core, usage of the DTI AAS Viewer or full AASX file generation. This endpoint contains the following struct in the request body Usage Request Connector | DTI Documentation Version: 1.0.11 https://docs.neoception.dev/connector/index.html 16 / 28

# Page 17^page=17
Array of propertyIds with values which do not require the specification of languages. The contained propertyIds are a subset of what the Connector previously offered the DTI Core using the response content of the GET /model call. PropertiesWithoutLanguage.propertyIds public class PropertyValuesRequest { /// < summary > /// List of properties which value will change based on language /// </ summary > public PropertiesWithLanguage PropertiesWithLanguage { get ; set ; } = new (); /// < summary > /// List of properties which value won't change based on language /// </ summary > public PropertiesWithoutLanguage PropertiesWithoutLanguage { get ; set ; } = new (); } public class PropertiesWithLanguage { /// < summary > /// First item in the list represents the selected language in the Viewer /// Second to last items in the list are the fallback languages sorted by priority /// If empty, all languages available should be sent /// /// ISO 639 language codes should be used /// </ summary > public List < string >? Languages { get ; set ; } = new (); public List < string >? PropertyIds { get ; set ; } = new (); } public class PropertiesWithoutLanguage { public List < string >? PropertyIds { get ; set ; } = new (); } c# Connector | DTI Documentation Version: 1.0.11 https://docs.neoception.dev/connector/index.html 17 / 28

# Page 18^page=18
The Connector should also accept null and an empty array [] as valid input. Array of propertyIds with values that are expected to change depending on the specified language mixed with propertyIds representing documents (files). The contained propertyIds are a subset of what the Connector previously offered the DTI Core using the response content of the GET /model call. The Connector should also accept null and an empty array [] as valid input. Referenced properties should have textual property values like "low-active", "Class 2", "clocked" etc. The language dependency of a textual property is not decided by the data source system or the Connector. Instead, this depends on the associated Submodel Element Type (MultiLanguageProperty ) in the target structure of the AAS currently being generated. As of version 3.1.2, the AAS meta model doesn't explicitly support specifying if a document is language dependent or not. As a consequence, the DTI provides all document properties, no matter if their content value is in fact language dependent or not, in the PropertiesWithLanguage.propertyIds array and the Connector needs to step in to make this decision. If the Connector is able to determine that a document doesn't have any language dependency like e.g. a pictographic logo or a schematic drawing without any text, it may return a single document property value without language tag. Should the content of a document be language dependent, the same logic applies as for Natural Language Properties. Array of language identification tags like specified by the Internet Engineering Task Force in the Best Common Practice document 47. Generally speaking, it is not necessary to implement methods for parsing all code formats. However, 2-letter codes (e.g. "en", "de" and "pt") and language + region pairs (e.g. "en-US", "pt-PT") must be supported. PropertiesWithLanguage.propertyIds Natural Language Properties Document Properties PropertiesWithLanguage.languages Connector | DTI Documentation Version: 1.0.11 https://docs.neoception.dev/connector/index.html 18 / 28

# Page 19^page=19
The Connector should also accept null and an empty array [] as valid input, in this case the Connector should query all existing property value + language tag combinations which are available in the data source system(s) and add all of them to the result array. If this array in fact contains language tags, the Connector should try to query the property value matching the first language tag. On success, it should add this property value with its language tag to the result array and proceed to the next propertyId . If the first language tag doesn't lead to success, the Connector should retry using the second and so on, until a single property value is found and added to the result array. In other words, when concrete language tag values are provided there is need to retrieve more than one property value, the language tags can be interpreted as preferred language followed by fallback languages to show the user the best possible text value. A typical use cases for this behavior is the visualization of an AAS in web-based Viewers where the user can select his preferred language in the browser. When there are no more language tags left to try, either just skip this propertyId and do not add any data for it to the result array or fall back to e.g. the English ("en") language tag and add this result anyway. Each Connector implementor should check with his business colleagues which options fit best to their needs. Connector | DTI Documentation Version: 1.0.11 https://docs.neoception.dev/connector/index.html 19 / 28

# Page 20^page=20
200 OK with List<ProductProperty> . Request example: Response { "propertiesWithLanguage" : { "languages" : [ "de" , "en" ], "propertyIds" : [ "PIM_Thumbnail" , "PIM_Website" , "PIM_ManufacturerProductDesignation" , "MarkingFile1" , "MarkingFile2" ] }, "propertiesWithoutLanguage" : { "propertyIds" : [] } } public class ProductProperty { public string PropertyId { get ; set ; } public string ? Value { get ; set ; } public string ? MimeType { get ; set ; } public string ? FileName { get ; set ; } public ulong ? Size { get ; set ; } public string ? ValueLanguage { get ; set ; } public List < string >? Languages { get ; set ; } /// < summary > /// Whether a reference needs to be turned into the actual document by using GetDocuments /// </ summary > public bool NeedsResolve { get ; set ; } } json c# Connector | DTI Documentation Version: 1.0.11 https://docs.neoception.dev/connector/index.html 20 / 28

# Page 21^page=21
References a propertyId given in PropertiesWithoutLanguage.propertyIds or PropertiesWithLanguage.propertyIds . In this response array, multiple ProductProperty objects may share the same propertyId value, as long as they differ in their valueLanguage value. This is the intended way of returning all available value + language tag tuples as reaction to an empty PropertiesWithLanguage.languages array. Holds the property value uniformly as string. Setting value to null is equivalent as not providing the ProductProperty object at all. Textual property values can immediately be assigned to value without any conversion. Values of all other data types need to be converted to a string representation. The section Enumeration for Submodel Element Value Types of the AAS Meta model specification provides a good guideline for the implementation of this conversion. Especially "Table 3� Data Types with Examples" provides concrete examples for numerous data types - considering that the non-textual values should be seen here as strings as well, e.g. "234.567e8" for xs:float or "true" and "false" for xs:boolean . For properties that represent documents, the Value field may contain the Base64 encoded string of the value, a URL pointing to the resource or a ticker to be used for document redemption at a later point in time. See Document Values Retrieval for more details. ProductProperty.propertyId ProductProperty.value Textual Property Values Other Data Types Document Property Values Connector | DTI Documentation Version: 1.0.11 https://docs.neoception.dev/connector/index.html 21 / 28

# Page 22^page=22
Holds the language tag correlating with the language of value if applicable. Refer to PropertiesWithLanguage.languages for the language tag specification. Should relate to a language tag provided in PropertiesWithLanguage.languages , if available. Only required for language dependent strings and documents. May be null or omitted for language independent values like numeric values, specific textual values (like e.g. "Android", "HTTPS" or "IEC 60529") and image file documents. Specifies the document content type, if value holds a document. Should be absent or null for all other kind of data in value . A comprehensive list of media types can e.g. be found at the Internet Assigned Numbers Authority. Only required for document properties, can be null or omitted for all other data types. When transforming a document into a file, it needs a name. The value provided in this property is the preferred name. To ensure maximum compatibility, the provided value should only contain a conservative set of characters, consisting of letters A-Z and a-z , numbers 0-9 , hyphen - , underscore _ and period . . If used at all, the period character should only be present once to separate file name and extension. fileName does not need to specify a file extension, it is automatically derived from mimeType and appended. Sould a file extension be provided, it will be overwritten by the file extension defined by mimeType . If fileName is omitted or null , the fallback is a derivative of the idShort of the target AAS submodel element to which the document was assigned, supported by the value provided in mimeType for the file extension. Typical use cases for file creation are document downloads in the Neoception DTI AAS Viewer and the creation of an AASX file. Optional field. Can be used to communicate the size of documents not directly embedded on the response. ProductProperty.valueLanguage ProductProperty.mimeType ProductProperty.fileName ProductProperty.size Connector | DTI Documentation Version: 1.0.11 https://docs.neoception.dev/connector/index.html 22 / 28

# Page 23^page=23
If not fully implemented or for properties which are not documents, should always contain 0 . Optional field. Can be used to communicate all the languages for which a document is available. All these languages, including the one whose value is inside valueLanguage should be listed here. If not fully implemented or for properties which are not documents, should always contain [] . Set to true for document values if their contents is to be retrieved following the By Ticket With Subsequent Redemption method. Omit or set to false in all other cases. When true , the DTI will use the call POST /product/{itemId}/documents retrieve the actual document content. The DTI will receive two separate object instances of type ProductProperty , one from the POST /product/{itemId}/values call and another from the POST /product/{itemId}/documents call. In both ProductProperty objects for this document, the common properties valueLanguage , mimeType and fileName should have the same value. ProductProperty.languages ProductProperty.needsResolve Connector | DTI Documentation Version: 1.0.11 https://docs.neoception.dev/connector/index.html 23 / 28

# Page 24^page=24
Response example: [ { "propertyId" : "PIM_Thumbnail" , // Document that already brings the content, needsResolve=False "value" : "v8Y8XRaESWm4U1p...hh71CsYzYAAAAASUVORK5CYII=" , "mimeType" : "image/png" , "filename" : "284586-100008" , "valueLanguage" : "de" , "languages" : [ "en" , "de" ], "needsResolve" : false }, { "propertyId" : "PIM_Website" , // The returned language is en because there was no value for de. You requested the properties for ["de", "en"]. "value" : "https://www.pepperl-fuchs.com/de-de/products- gp25581/107064" , "valueLanguage" : "en" , "needsResolve" : false }, { "propertyId" : "PIM_ManufacturerProductDesignation" , "value" : "Laserlichtschnitt-Sensor" , "valueLanguage" : "de" , "needsResolve" : false }, { "propertyId" : "MarkingFile1" , // Document that the content-type is a URL "value" : "http://name.pdf" , "mimeType" : "image/png" , "filename" : "MarkingFile1" , "valueLanguage" : "de" , "languages" : [ "en" , "de" ], "needsResolve" : false }, { "propertyId" : "MarkingFile2" , // Document that brings a reference that needs to be redeemed. needsResolve=true json Connector | DTI Documentation Version: 1.0.11 https://docs.neoception.dev/connector/index.html 24 / 28

# Page 25^page=25
HTTP Method: POST Endpoint(Post): https://your-service/product/{itemId}/documents This endpoint is used to retrive document data for By Ticket With Subsequent Redemption document retrieval. In this case it is the logical continuation after POST /product/{itemId}/values . Provide the option of fast and efficient UI rendering in the DTI AAS Viewer by delaying requesting the document content until it is in fact required. The DTI AAS Viewer supports to just describe a document in a first step using the document meta data provided in the response content of POST /product/{itemId}/values . To keep track of the document, its property value is kept as a form of "ticket". This endpoint contains the following struct in the request body Product Documents Motivation Usage Request "value" : "DocRef0001" , "mimeType" : "application/pdf" , "fileName" : "MarkingFile2" , "languages" : [ "en" , "de" ], "needsResolve" : true } ] Connector | DTI Documentation Version: 1.0.11 https://docs.neoception.dev/connector/index.html 25 / 28

# Page 26^page=26
Array of propertyIds with document "ticket" values. See the description of PropertiesWithLanguage.languages of the POST /product/{assetId}/values call. 200 OK with List<ProductProperty> . PropertiesWithLanguage.propertyIds PropertiesWithLanguage.languages Response public class PropertiesWithLanguage { /// < summary > /// First item in the list represents the selected language in the Viewer /// Second to last items in the list are the fallback languages sorted by priority /// If empty, all languages available should be sent /// </ summary > public List < string >? Languages { get ; set ; } = new (); public List < string >? PropertyIds { get ; set ; } = new (); } { "languages" : [ "en" ], "propertyIds" : [ "DocRef0001" ] } c# json Connector | DTI Documentation Version: 1.0.11 https://docs.neoception.dev/connector/index.html 26 / 28

# Page 27^page=27
HTTP Method: GET Endpoint(Post): https://your-service/product/ids This optional endpoint is used to retrieve the itemId of all products provided by the connector. All item IDs public class ProductProperty { public string PropertyId { get ; set ; } public string ? Value { get ; set ; } public string ? MimeType { get ; set ; } public string ? FileName { get ; set ; } public ulong ? Size { get ; set ; } public string ? ValueLanguage { get ; set ; } public List < string >? Languages { get ; set ; } /// < summary > /// Whether a reference needs to be turned into the actual document by using GetDocuments /// </ summary > public bool NeedsResolve { get ; set ; } } [ { "propertyId" : "DocRef0001" , "value" : "JVBERi0...KJHDH" , // Content in base64 "fileName" : "DocRef0001.pdf" , "needsResolve" : false } ] c# json Connector | DTI Documentation Version: 1.0.11 https://docs.neoception.dev/connector/index.html 27 / 28

# Page 28^page=28
Some additional apps, most notably the Snapshot, have features that target every known item in the system. This call can be implemented to support these functions. If they are not desired, do not expose this endpoint. The response is a simple string array containing the gathered itemId s. Motivation Response [ "REFRI001" , "CBL001" , "RFID02" , "EV009" , "CBL005" , "CBL009" , "REFRI004" , "RFID04" , "CBL002" , "RFID06" ] json Connector | DTI Documentation Version: 1.0.11 https://docs.neoception.dev/connector/index.html 28 / 28

# Page 29^page=29
If a ProductProperty.value is intended to represent a document (file), Connector developers can choose between three methods to provide the document content. Retrieval method Allowed for images value contents needsResolve By Reference YES Publicly accessible document URL false or omitted By Value YES Base64-encoded document content, supported only for image documents false or omitted By Ticket NO Internal ticket or identifier that can later be redeemed through POST /product/{itemId}/documents true Images Retrieval As of the current DTI version, images must use either the By Value or By Reference retrieval method. This is scheduled to be relaxed in future versions of the DTI. This is the simplest option, value just holds a document URL pointing to the referenced document. The URL should be publicly and anonymously accessible, otherwise the option By Ticket With Subsequent Redemption is recommended. Set needsResolve to false or omit it, the document content can be retrieved from another server, therefore no subsequent call to this Connector is required. Document Value Retrieval By Reference Document Value Retrieval | DTI Documentation Version: 1.0.11 https://docs.neoception.dev/connector/document-value-retrieval.html 1 / 2

# Page 30^page=30
value directly holds the base64 encoded string representation of the document content. Set needsResolve to false or omit it, the document content is already present, therefore no subsequent call to this Connector is required. In the method, the transmission of the actual document content is deferred. The value field just holds a preliminary "ticket" used for document retrieval. Additionally, needsResolve must be set to true to identify that ticket retrieval is The "ticket" value is an identifier string that is intended for internal use, it will not be part of the generated AAS. However, it should be enough for the POST /product/{itemId}/documents to be able to identify the exact document requested. If at a later point in time the actual document content is required, this "ticket" will be provided to the call POST /product/{itemId}/documents as a propertyIds array element. Set needsResolve to true to inform the DTI accordingly. This option can e.g. implement the scenario that a primary data source system holds URLs or other kind of identifying values pointing to a secondary, protected and internal document management system. The URL could become the "ticket" value for the subsequent call to POST /product/{itemId}/documents . In less complex scenarios, the "ticket" value could simply be the propertyId of the document. This method also supports reducing data traffic in certain use cases by delaying the transmission of the document content to when it is actually required. By Value By Ticket Document Value Retrieval | DTI Documentation Version: 1.0.11 https://docs.neoception.dev/connector/document-value-retrieval.html 2 / 2

# Page 31^page=31
Connectors can use whatever authentication method it is necessary to access the customer's systems and retrieve the data for requests. The implementation details vary according to the system being accessed and the requirements of the institution managing it. It is the job of the connector to abstract away such concerns from the perspective of the DTI core. Connectors that require the DTI to authenticate to access them is a planned feature for the DTI 1.2. The following authentication approaches are supported for DTI to connector communications: OpenID Connect via client credentials Basic HTTP authentication Users of such connectors must provide the required information for the DTI to access them. This either comes in the form of Username � Password for basic authentication or token endpoint/discovery endpoint, client ID, client secret and scope for OpenID authentication. Connector Authentication Connector to customer system authentication DTI to connector authentication Connector Authentication | DTI Documentation Version: 1.0.11 https://docs.neoception.dev/connector/authentication.html 1 / 1
